concepts.title: Smooth manifolds and atlases
concepts.blurb: Smooth manifolds glue Euclidean charts by smooth transition maps.

concepts.title: Examples: spheres, projective spaces, matrix groups
concepts.blurb: S^n, RP^n, tori, Grassmannians, and classical matrix groups are the canonical manifolds to have in hand before doing anything intrinsic.

concepts.title: Smooth maps and diffeomorphisms
concepts.blurb: A map is smooth when every chart representative is C^∞; diffeomorphisms are the isomorphisms of the smooth category.

concepts.title: Tangent spaces
concepts.blurb: T_pM has three equivalent definitions — equivalence classes of curves, chart velocities, and derivations of C^∞(M) — all naturally isomorphic.

concepts.title: Tangent bundle and vector fields
concepts.blurb: TM assembles all tangent spaces into a rank-n vector bundle; smooth sections are vector fields and act as derivations on C^∞(M).

concepts.title: Lie bracket of vector fields
concepts.blurb: [X,Y] = XY−YX is a vector field; it measures the failure of flows to commute and gives 𝔛(M) a Lie algebra structure.

concepts.title: Immersions, submersions, and submanifolds
concepts.blurb: Rank conditions on df control local models: immersions inject tangentially, submersions project, embeddings land as submanifolds; Whitney says every n-manifold embeds in R^{2n}.

concepts.title: Integral curves and flows
concepts.blurb: A vector field X integrates to a flow Phi^X_t: the fundamental theorem gives a unique maximal integral curve through each point, complete when M is compact.

concepts.title: Partitions of unity
concepts.blurb: Subordinate partitions of unity exist on any smooth manifold and are the standard device for gluing local constructions into global ones.

concepts.title: Orientation of manifolds
concepts.blurb: An orientation is a coherent choice of sign on the top exterior power of every tangent space; equivalently, an atlas whose transitions have positive Jacobian.
